creator: salvadore@freebsd.org
text:
This is a known problem due to the LTO_BOOTSTRAP option: lto requires significant amounts of memory and time. I do not know the details of how it works so I do not know why things are worse with 12 cpus rather than 8: maybe more optimization is possible with more cores and this means more resources are needed to compute the best optimization.

If what you want is to deploy a simple desktop, then you most probably do not need any bootstraping. In that case you can simply disable both LTO_BOOTSTRAP and STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP option, which will give you much faster compilations.

> Furthermore, I also think that now having to build 8 (eight!) different compilers in the process of a rather simple desktop deploy is quite insane - but that's not directly related to this issue.

This is a problem about which we are well aware and we do our best to improve things. As far as GCC is concerned, I do my best to ensure that only GCC_DEFAULT version is needed (at the moment GCC_DEFAULT=12), although a few ports (usually older ones), might need an older version to build. I also do my best to reduce the number of ports depending on GCC.
